items.text:
Dear Student,
Your replacement student ID is ready for pickup at the Campus Card Office in Anderson Hall. A $10 fee is required before issuance. Please bring your current photo ID and pay by cash or card during business hours.
Best regards,
Campus Card Services

questions.stem: According to the email, what must a student do before receiving a replacement ID?

options.A: To provide a photo ID for verification
options.B: To visit the Campus Card Office after hours
options.C: To submit an online application form
options.D: To pay a fee of ten dollars in cash or by card

questions.explanation: The email states 'A $10 fee is required before issuance' and 'pay by cash or card', so paying the fee is necessary.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the replacement ID process?

options.A: The student must have a previous ID to get a replacement
options.B: The fee can be waived for students with financial need
options.C: The original ID is likely lost or damaged
options.D: The office requires an appointment for pickup

questions.explanation: The email refers to 'replacement student ID,' which implies the original ID is unavailable, typically due to loss or damage.

items.text:
Free Flu Shot Clinic!
Protect yourself this season. Get your free flu shot on October 15, from 10:00 AM to 2:00 PM, at the Student Health Center, Room 104. No appointment needed. Bring your student ID. For questions, call 555-0199.

questions.stem: According to the flyer, what is required to receive a flu shot?

options.A: A prior appointment with the health center
options.B: A payment of five dollars for the vaccine
options.C: A student identification card be brought
options.D: A signed consent form from a parent

questions.explanation: The flyer states 'Bring your student ID,' indicating it is required.

questions.stem: Where will the flu shot clinic take place?

options.A: Room 104 at the Student Health Center
options.B: The main lobby of Anderson Hall
options.C: A mobile unit near the library
options.D: The Campus Card Office in South Lot B

questions.explanation: The flyer specifies 'at the Student Health Center, Room 104.'

items.text:
Hi Alex,
Your library books are due this Friday, March 10. Please return them to the front desk in the Main Library by 5:00 PM. A late fee of $0.25 per day applies. Don't forget to check your account for any holds.
Thanks,
Library Staff

questions.stem: According to the email, what action does the student need to take?

options.A: To return library books by Friday afternoon
options.B: To renew books online before the deadline
options.C: To pay a late fee of twenty-five cents
options.D: To check for holds on a different account

questions.explanation: The email says 'Please return them to the front desk... by 5:00 PM' on Friday, March 10.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the student's library account?

options.A: The student has no overdue books at this time
options.B: The student may have other items on hold
options.C: The student has already paid the late fee
options.D: The student must visit the library after hours

questions.explanation: The email advises to 'check your account for any holds,' implying there might be holds present.

items.text:
Dear Student,
My office hours this week have changed. I will be available on Wednesday, May 15, from 2:00 PM to 4:00 PM in Room 302. Please bring your draft essay for review. A $5 printing fee applies if you need materials.
Best,
Dr. Park

questions.stem: When can the student meet with Dr. Park this week?

options.A: On Friday, May 17, from 9:00 AM to 11:00 AM
options.B: On Monday, May 13, at 2:00 PM in Room 302
options.C: On Thursday, May 16, from 10:00 AM to noon
options.D: On Wednesday, May 15, between 2:00 and 4:00 PM

questions.explanation: The email states 'on Wednesday, May 15, from 2:00 PM to 4:00 PM.'

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the student's situation?

options.A: The student has already completed a draft essay
options.B: The student may need to pay a fee for printing
options.C: The student prefers to meet in the morning hours
options.D: The student must schedule a separate appointment

questions.explanation: Dr. Park mentions 'A $5 printing fee applies if you need materials,' implying printing might be needed.
